title: Curriculum extractor reads the STALE draft tabs, not the colored authoritative tab
description:
extract_curriculum.py's content_sheets() only reads sheets named 'Sheet1*' and explicitly discards 'Sheet2' as a 'stale draft'. That assumption is INVERTED. The authoritative, corrected table is the COLOR-FILLED tab — Sheet2 for levels 3-10, Sheet1 for levels 1-2. Every 'source data defect' filed under al_rasikhoon-drw (surah typos, duplicate lessons, verse drift, inverted ranges, the هود 29-123 gap) is an artefact of reading the abandoned draft tabs; the colored tabs are clean.

VERIFIED across all 48 workbooks:
- Authoritative tab = the one with the most color-filled cells (200-900 fills vs ~3 on drafts). No xlsx tabColor is set anywhere, so selection must be by cell fill.
- Colored-tab layout DIFFERS from the drafts: header sits ~row 7/8 (not 4/5), and the سرد/اختبار marker text lives in a SEPARATE column (col P, merged P:U) instead of inline in the content columns.
- Combining the workbooks of a juz folder:
  * L1, L8, L9, L10 — single workbook; take its colored tab.
  * L4, L5, L6, L7 — two workbooks, colored tabs hold DISJOINT halves of one continuous numbering (e.g. 1-15 + 16-31) -> merge by session number.
  * L2, L3 — two workbooks, each colored tab renumbers from scratch (e.g. 2-18 + 2-17, content differs) -> concatenate in hizb order.

Session counts will change (currently 672 rows). Regenerating data/curriculum/*.json changes session IDs, so student positions stored in Firestore may need migrating — track separately.
close_reason: Fixed: extractor now selects the colour-filled authoritative tab by fill count and detects the four juz-assembly layouts. Data regenerated (952 -> 955 sessions). Follow-ups filed for the position migration and the 4 remaining source typos.

title: [bug] Any authenticated user can read EVERY student's record and progress — students/records are 'allow read: if isAuthenticated()'
description:
firestore.rules grants blanket read to every authenticated user across the data model:
  match /students/{studentId} { allow read: if isAuthenticated(); }
and the same 'allow read: if isAuthenticated()' on the record collections (session records, sard, exam — rules lines ~63, 82, 88, 112, 117, 125, 147, 155, 162).

Consequences:
- Any signed-in STUDENT can read every other student's record and full progress history.
- Any signed-in GUARDIAN can read every student in the system, not just their own children.
- Cross-institute leakage: the careful institute scoping on WRITES (isSupervisorOfStudentInstitute, AgDR-0003) has no read-side counterpart, so a supervisor can read students of other institutes.

The restrictions that supposedly enforce this are CLIENT-SIDE QUERY FILTERS ONLY — e.g. getStudentsByGuardianId does .where('guardian_id', isEqualTo: ...), and getStudentsForInstitute does .where('institute_id', isEqualTo: ...). A where() clause is a filter, NOT an access control boundary: it shapes what the app asks for, not what the backend will hand over. Anything holding a valid ID token can request the rest directly.

Note: the word 'guardian' appears ZERO times in firestore.rules — the guardian↔child relationship (students.guardian_id) is entirely unenforced server-side.

Fix: scope reads by role —
- guardian → only students where guardian_id == request.auth.uid (and their records)
- student → only their own record
- teacher → their students
- supervisor → students of their institute(s) — coordinate with al_rasikhoon-3n6, which changes how supervisor institute membership is resolved
- admin → all
Records (session/sard/exam) must be scoped via the denormalized student institute_id / student_id they already carry.

Add rules tests proving each role CANNOT read what it should not — a passing app is not evidence, because the app never asks for the forbidden data.

title: Re-import curriculum to Firestore with new schema (purge old L_J_H_S docs)
description: firebase_import.js: delete its getSessionType(session_number) which OVERWRITES the extractor's type; add --purge of the sessions collection (doc IDs change from L{l}_J{j}_H{h}_S{n} to L{l}_J{j}_S{n}, so stale docs would survive every level_id/juz_number query); import the new levels shape carrying per-juz session counts.

title: App: session identity becomes (level, juz, session); type comes from data
description: Domain: CurriculumPosition -> (level, juz, session), drop the 1..36 range check; CurriculumOrder gains nextJuz/juzOrderIndex, hizb functions demoted to level-aware labels. Models: SessionKind{lesson,sard,exam} + tier/scope/unitIndex/assessedBy; StudentModel.canTakeSard/canTakeExam read the denormalized current session kind. Repos: getSessionNumbersForJuz; _nextPosition walks sessions->juz->level; sard/exam records gain a scope; unlimited retries for assessments. Exam queue stays one Firestore query by denormalizing current_session_kind onto the student doc, written only via a single _writePosition(SessionModel). UI: five duplicated isSard/isExam derivations, hardcoded hizb labels in the Sard/Exam flows, four /36 progress sites, and the picker becomes Level->Juz->Session.
close_reason: Implemented. CurriculumPosition=(level,juz,session) in lib/domain/curriculum/curriculum_position.dart; SessionKind{talqeen,lesson,sard,exam} in session_model.dart; StudentModel.canTakeSard/canTakeExam read denormalized currentSessionKind; curriculum_repository.getSessionNumbersForJuz; _writePosition(SessionModel) is sole writer of current_* denormalized fields; starting_point_picker is Level->Juz->Session; no 1..36 range checks or /36 progress sites remain (only comments describing the removed pattern).

title: Rewrite curriculum extractor: faithful per-juz sessions, tiers from structure, no fabrication
description: Move extract_curriculum.py into the app repo (tools/curriculum/) with a venv + requirements.txt. Rewrite it: session numbers dense 1..N per juz in teaching order; kind (lesson|sard|exam) and assessed_by read from the row's Arabic text, never from the number; tier (unit|juz|cumulative) derived structurally from consecutive sard/exam pairs and cross-checked against the text; sheet selection by rule with abort-on-conflict; delete get_session_type() and ensure_sard_and_exam_sessions(); every emitted session carries a source row (file/sheet/row); validation gate that refuses to write on any anomaly. Golden tests: L1 juz 30 (merged, ascending), L2 juz 27 (split halves, descending), L3 juz 22 (surah-scoped assessments).

title: Curriculum session model is wrong: per-juz numbering + three assessment tiers
description: Epic. The app models hizb->36 sessions with 35=Sard/36=Exam. The real manhaj numbers sessions continuously across a JUZ (juz 30 has 68, juz 29 has 69, juz 28 has 67) and has Sard+Exam at THREE tiers: per hizb, per juz, per level. Teaching order within a juz: level 1 ascends (59 then 60), levels 2-10 descend (54 then 53). The seeded Firestore data is also fabricated: extract_curriculum.py types sessions by number and ensure_sard_and_exam_sessions() invents placeholder Sard/Exam rows. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/linked-seeking-firefly.md. Reworks PR #56 before merge.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #56 (3c02eb4) + follow-ups. extract_curriculum.py now uses per-juz numbering with level-aware teaching order and never infers type from session number; ensure_sard_and_exam_sessions() removed; three assessment tiers modeled; data regenerated from authoritative colour-filled tabs.

title: Pace up to 10x + Quran completion forecast (متى الختم؟)
description: Supervisor/teacher can set memorization pace 1..10 (slider UI, not 10 buttons). New meetings-per-week student config (1..7, default 2). New completion forecast: remaining meetings from current position at pace N (mirrors PacedSessionComposer batching), divided by meetings/week → duration + expected date. Interactive what-if calculator card for all roles (student, teacher, supervisor, guardian, admin) + remaining-time on student home dashboard.

title: Supervisor stats/history and teacher session-count queries denied by exam/session record read rules
description: canReadRecord() scopes record reads through get(students/$(student_id)), which the rules engine cannot prove for list queries that do not pin student_id. So exam_records.where(supervisor_id==uid) (supervisorStatsProvider, supervisorExamHistoryProvider) and session_records.where(teacher_id==uid) (+count(), teacherStatsProvider, getSessionRecordsForTeacher) are permission-denied wholesale. Supervisor home stats never load (dimmed shimmer blocks in screenshot). Fix: add provable author-scoped read clauses — examiner (supervisor_id==uid) on exam_records, conducting teacher (teacher_id==uid) on session_records. Verified with @firebase/rules-unit-testing against the emulator.

title: User profile management + password self-service & admin reset
description: Self-service: every user can edit own name/phone and change own password (reauth + updatePassword). Admin: reset supervisor/teacher/student passwords and edit their name/phone from detail screens. Also fixes users self-update rule that errors on docs without institute_id.

title: Implement curriculum-correct evaluation for سرد and اختبار sessions
description: Curriculum docs (curriculum/**/سرد*, اختبارات*) define assessment evaluation differently from lessons: errors are tracked across 4 types (تنبيهات/تلقينات/تشكيل/تجويد); سرد passes only if EVERY face stays within 5/2/1/8; اختبار has 5 questions each within 3/2/1/5; outcome is binary موفق/غير موفق — not the راسخ..محب grade tiers. Current code grades sard/exam with GradeCalculator.calculateForLevel like lessons, which is wrong. Add domain value objects for tallies/limits/evaluations, per-face and per-question storage on Sard/ExamRecordModel, repository wiring, and new session/result UI.
close_reason: Implemented curriculum-correct evaluation: domain layer lib/domain/assessment/assessment_evaluation.dart (4 error types, per-face sard limits 5/2/1/8, per-question exam limits 3/2/1/5 across 5 questions, binary موفق/غير موفق), per-face/per-question storage on records, repo + UI rework, 1065 tests green

title: Recitation part-ink redesign: illumination triad across grading flow
description: Replace Okabe-Ito raw part colors with tokenized green/ochre/lapis triad; part-colored heroes on recitation screens; profile polish

title: Design rollout P1: role homes (admin/supervisor/teacher) + shared surfaces
Unified session log: exams and sard missing from سجل الحلقات

Add StudentHistoryEntry domain read-model + SessionRepository.getStudentHistory merging all three; update 4 history providers + render sites. Assessment rows non-tappable (no detail screen). title: [bug] Backfill institute_id onto pre-existing students docs (supervisor setUserPassword/rules fail closed without it)
description: Surfaced by al_rasikhoon-fh2 audit (PR #98). Commit 51c0dcb deferred backfilling institute_id onto existing students docs. The supervisor branch of setUserPassword (and the sibling firestore.rules institute-scoping) FAIL CLOSED for any student doc missing institute_id — so a legitimate supervisor cannot reset a real student's password (or otherwise act) on students created before the field existed, until backfilled. Write a one-off backfill setting institute_id on all pre-existing students docs; verify the older records before/after.

title: [bug] Teachers can create/edit ANY student document (students write is bare isTeacher())
description: Surfaced during al_rasikhoon-ob7 (PR #87). ob7 scoped teacher writes on session_records/sard_records to the teacher's own students, but the /students collection create+update rules are still bare isTeacher(), so any teacher can create or edit ANY student's document (including students of another teacher/institute) — repointing teacher_id, moving enrollment, etc. Distinct from ob7 (records). Fix: scope teacher students writes via isTeacherOfStudent()/institute, with a dual old+new check on update, mirroring the record rules. Add deny/allow rules tests.

title: [bug] home_practices is still world-readable (allow read: if isAuthenticated())
description: Follow-up from al_rasikhoon-bpk (PR #83). While bpk scoped reads on /students, /session_records, /sard_records, /exam_records by role, the /home_practices collection was outside bpk's listed line set and remains 'allow read: if isAuthenticated()'. It holds per-student home-practice data, so any signed-in user can read every student's home practice across institutes — the same class of leak bpk fixed. Fix: scope home_practices reads via canReadRecord()/canReadStudent() (or an equivalent per its denormalized student_id/institute_id), mirroring bpk, and add deny/allow rules tests in test/rules/firestore.rules.test.js.

title: Fast startup via optimistic session restore
description: Seed appUser from a Hive SessionCache in AuthRepository.build() so returning users route instantly (no Firestore gate), reconcile in background, and replace the blank white launch with a branded splash + logo. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-15-fast-startup-optimistic-session.md

title: Migrate stored student positions after the curriculum re-extraction (levels 3-10)
description: al_rasikhoon-hk0 re-extracted the curriculum from the authoritative colour-filled tabs. Levels 1-2 are byte-identical, but levels 3-10 changed: session counts moved (L3 99->100, L4 93->94, L10 49->50) and ~85% of the rows that keep their id now carry different content. Any student position stored in Firestore for levels 3-10 points at a session that has moved. Needs a migration plan before the new data/curriculum/*.json is imported to a live environment.
notes:
MOOT — closed without a migration. Production Firestore held only test data (confirmed by the owner), so no real student position referenced a renumbered session id. The new curriculum was imported straight to production on 2026-07-14: purged 952 stale session docs, wrote 955 sessions + 10 levels. Verified by read-back: 0 stale L_J_H_S ids, 0 inverted ranges, 0 sessions missing provenance, 0 assessments carrying lesson content.

If real students ever exist BEFORE the next curriculum re-import, this issue's original concern comes back and a real migration is required.

title: [bug] main's integration suite is red: 3 teacher session tests + 3 supervisor exam tests fail
description:
Verified by running the integration suites on a clean worktree at origin/main (08e00bc):

teacher_flow_test.dart — FAIL:
- Teacher can conduct a session with passing grade
- Teacher can conduct a session with failing grade
- Failed session allows student to retry

supervisor_flow_test.dart — FAIL:
- Supervisor can conduct exam with passing result
- Supervisor can conduct exam with failing result
- a supervisor conducts a JUZ-tier اختبار from the queue

These are NOT caused by al_rasikhoon-801 (PR #61): that branch fails exactly the same 6 and no others, i.e. it is at parity with main.

The most likely cause is the تلقين PR (#60, 08e00bc), which renumbered the curriculum sessions — the same renumbering that silently invalidated every Sard session id in the al_rasikhoon-801 tests (L1_J30_S30 became a lesson, S67 became the hizb-60 سرد, S66 became a juz-tier سرد). The teacher's regular-session tests and the supervisor's exam tests very likely name session ids that now mean something else, or land on a تلقين where they expect a graded lesson.

Also note: whatever CI exists is evidently not gating on the integration suite, or this would not have merged. Worth fixing that too.

Also flaky: 'Teacher can add a new student' failed once and passed on a clean re-run.

title: [bug] Supervisor cannot finish an exam: the exam session screen overflows and hides the finish button
description:
lib/features/supervisor/screens/exam_session_screen.dart:51 builds an unscrollable Column. On a 390x651 viewport (iPhone 16e) it overflows by 45px, pushing the finish action off-screen: 'A RenderFlex overflowed by 45 pixels on the bottom.'

The supervisor therefore cannot complete an exam on a normal-sized phone — the flow dead-ends at the exam session screen and never reaches نتيجة الاختبار.

Evidence: three integration tests in integration_test/supervisor_flow_test.dart fail on this:
- 'Supervisor can conduct exam with passing result'
- 'Supervisor can conduct exam with failing result'
- 'a supervisor conducts a JUZ-tier اختبار from the queue...'
All three fail at SupervisorRobot.verifyExamResult with 'Found 0 widgets with text containing نتيجة'.

PRE-EXISTING AND UNRELATED to al_rasikhoon-801: verified by running the same suite at the branch merge-base (9bc8359, main) and at 042aefa — the same 3 tests fail identically. main is red here.

Fix: make the body scrollable (SingleChildScrollView, as SardSessionScreen effectively achieves with its Spacer/Column layout) or constrain the flex children. Then the three tests above should pass without modification — they are correct tests catching a real bug.

Note this means the supervisor exam E2E has been red on main; whatever CI exists is not running (or not gating on) the integration suite.

title: [bug] A supervisor-created student has no teacher, so nobody can conduct their sessions
description:
AddStudentScreen(asSupervisor: true) writes teacher_id: null (lib/features/teacher/screens/add_student_screen.dart:180). Such a student is returned by NO teacher's getStudentsForTeacher query, so they never appear in any teacher's الطلاب list.

Consequence today: nobody can conduct that student's regular حلقة — the teacher cannot see them, and the supervisor has no session action. This is why SessionOverviewScreen needed its asSupervisor lookup at all.

Consequence after al_rasikhoon-801 (Sard becomes teacher-only): they can never have a سرد conducted either. The swap does not cause this bug, it exposes it.

Fix is student->teacher assignment, not the Sard swap. Options to weigh:
- require the supervisor to pick a teacher from the institute at creation, and/or
- let a supervisor (re)assign a student to a teacher afterwards, and/or
- surface teacher-less students somewhere they can be claimed.

Discovered while designing al_rasikhoon-801; see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-14-sard-teacher-exam-supervisor-design.md.

title: السرد becomes teacher-conducted; الاختبار stays supervisor-conducted (reverses #29)
description:
Exclusive swap. Sard (السرد) is conducted by the TEACHER; Exam (الاختبار) stays with the SUPERVISOR. A supervisor can no longer start a Sard; a teacher still cannot start an Exam.

This reverses the Sard half of #29. Exams already work as intended and are untouched.

Scope (see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-14-sard-teacher-exam-supervisor-design.md):
- Routing: /supervisor/sard/:studentId -> /teacher/session/:studentId/sard (+ /result). Router redirect guard flips: bounce non-TEACHERS off the Sard paths.
- Screens: sard_session_screen.dart and sard_result_screen.dart move features/supervisor/screens -> features/teacher/screens and swap supervisor-scoped providers for teacher-scoped ones. SessionOverviewScreen loses _buildSardSupervisorOnlyMessage, the isSupervisorProvider check, and its asSupervisor flag — a teacher on a sard session just gets 'بدء السرد'.
- Supervisor keeps its الطلاب tab (#28 roster management) but loses the session-overview twin: /supervisor/students/:studentId now renders a read-only progress screen (AdminStudentProgressScreen promoted to a shared StudentProgressScreen taking its providers as constructor params, wired by the router).
- Firestore rules: sard_records create/update becomes isTeacher(); supervisor Sard write removed. exam_records unchanged.
- Tests: the existing suite pins the OLD rule, so inverting the assertions IS the red step. firestore.rules.test.js #29 block turns inside out (teacher ALLOWED, supervisor DENIED, cross-institute supervisor-sard cases deleted); teacher_flow_test 'Teacher is blocked from Sard' becomes a full teacher Sard E2E; supervisor_flow_test 'Supervisor conducts a Sard' becomes 'Supervisor cannot conduct a Sard'; Sard robot methods move SupervisorRobot -> TeacherRobot.

Note: SardRecordModel already names its author teacher_id ('A سرد a student recited to their teacher') — #29 was writing a supervisor uid into a field called teacher_id. This puts model and rule back in agreement.

No data migration: historical sard records keep whoever actually conducted them.

title: Supervisors belong to MANY institutes: retire users/{uid}.institute_id as the scoping source of truth
description:
DECISION: a supervisor gets full teacher parity — freely assigned to and unassigned from institutes, and may supervise SEVERAL at once. This retires the single-institute model (AgDR-0003), where users/{uid}.institute_id is the source of truth for all supervisor scoping. Membership in supervisor_institutes/{uid}_{instituteId} becomes the source of truth instead.

This is a DATA MODEL + SECURITY RULES change, not a UI change. It blocks the add/remove-supervisor UI in al_rasikhoon-o8s.

⚠️ PRIVILEGE ESCALATION — THE MAIN RISK.
firestore.rules today freezes a supervisor's own users/{uid}.role AND institute_id (lines ~68-76) precisely so a supervisor cannot self-promote or re-scope themselves to another institute. When scoping moves to supervisor_institutes, THAT GUARD MUST MOVE WITH IT: supervisor_institutes docs must be writable ONLY by admins. If a supervisor can create supervisor_institutes/{ownUid}_{anyInstitute}, they grant themselves that institute — the exact escalation the current rule blocks, relocated. Rules must be written and TESTED for this before anything else lands.

WHAT CHANGES:

1. firestore.rules
   - callerInstituteId() (a single get() of users/{uid}.institute_id) is replaced by a membership check. Because the doc id is {uid}_{instituteId}, this stays a cheap single-doc exists() with NO multi-hop read and no fan-out:
       exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/supervisor_institutes/$(request.auth.uid + '_' + studentData.institute_id))
   - Update every caller: supervisorOwnsStudent (student docs, ~line 42), the session/sard/exam record checks (~line 53, ~121-138).
   - supervisor_institutes: admin-write-only (see escalation warning above).
   - Keep freezing users/{uid}.role.

2. functions/src/index.ts
   - createUserAccount currently REQUIRES instituteId for role 'supervisor' and writes users/{uid}.institute_id + one supervisor_institutes doc. Decide: does creation still take an initial institute (probably yes, as a convenience) — but it must no longer be treated as THE institute.
   - The supervisor-caller provisioning check reads callerDoc.institute_id and rejects a supervisor with no institute. It must instead verify the TARGET institute is one the caller is assigned to.

3. Client providers
   - supervisorInstituteIdProvider returns a single id → becomes a set of institutes (getInstitutesForSupervisor already EXISTS and is already plural).
   - supervisorStudentsProvider calls getStudentsForInstitute(one institute) → must union across the supervisor's institutes. NOTE Firestore whereIn is capped (30 values); a supervisor with more institutes than that needs chunked queries.
   - al_rasikhoon-ecv's teachers/students lists must scope to the SET, not a single id.

4. Repository
   - assignSupervisorToInstitute / removeSupervisorFromInstitute already exist and already write supervisor_institutes — under the new model they become CORRECT as-is (no users.institute_id to desync from). They are only dangerous while the single field still rules.

5. MIGRATION
   - Backfill: every existing supervisor with users/{uid}.institute_id must have a matching supervisor_institutes doc. createUserAccount has written both since #28, so recent supervisors are fine — VERIFY the older ones before dropping the field.
   - Drop users/{uid}.institute_id for supervisors only AFTER rules + clients no longer read it. Note the field is also used for non-supervisor purposes — confirm before deleting anything.

Supersedes the single-institute assumption in AgDR-0003; that ADR needs updating or superseding.

title: Audit every functions/src change made between 2026-05-10 and 2026-07-14 — none of it ran in production
description:
Because firebase.json had no predeploy hook, 'firebase deploy --only functions' shipped the stale compiled functions/lib (last built 2026-05-10) on every deploy. Any change to functions/src merged between 2026-05-10 and 2026-07-14 was therefore NEVER live, even though deploys reported success. See al_rasikhoon-vep for the root cause.

The predeploy hook is now in place, and the 2026-07-14 deploy shipped current source — so this backlog of changes went live ALL AT ONCE, unexercised, in a single deploy.

Do:
- git log --since=2026-05-10 -- functions/src   (starting with 6054be7) to enumerate what was dormant.
- For each change, exercise the affected flow against production and confirm the behaviour — do not assume it works just because it deployed.
- Pay particular attention to anything that silently changes authorization or data writes, since those failed closed (or worse, silently wrong) for two months.

Preventive follow-up: nothing verifies that deployed functions match HEAD. Consider a post-deploy check. Related: al_rasikhoon-5j2 (audit allUsers invoker after every deploy) — same missing post-deploy verification gap.
notes: Audit + preventive check delivered in PR #98 (audit doc docs/audits/2026-07-15-...md with per-commit risk ratings HIGH/HIGH/MED-HIGH + 8-step manual prod-verification checklist; functions/scripts/verify-build-fresh.sh build-freshness check). REMAINING = maintainer step: run the prod-verification checklist against production (agent must not touch prod). Keep open until that's done.

title: [bug] Super admin cannot create a supervisor: 'Caller role super_admin cannot provision role supervisor'
description:
Repro: sign in as super admin, add a new supervisor (lib/features/admin/screens/add_supervisor_screen.dart). The createUserAccount callable rejects with permission-denied: "Caller role 'super_admin' cannot provision role 'supervisor'" (functions/src/index.ts:110).

This blocks the entire admin create-supervisor flow.

KEY OBSERVATION: the current source PERMITS this. ROLES_BY_CALLER (functions/src/index.ts:33) gives super_admin the set {supervisor, teacher, student, guardian}, and the client sends the literal 'supervisor' (add_supervisor_screen.dart:101), matching AppConstants.roleSupervisor. So local HEAD should allow it — the deployed behaviour contradicts the source.

Leading hypothesis: STALE DEPLOY. The super_admin → supervisor entry was added in commit 6054be7 (2026-05-24, 'feat(#27): admin create-supervisor flow'). The error text can only be produced by a build whose super_admin set lacks 'supervisor' — i.e. the pre-6054be7 matrix. Cloud Functions have most likely not been deployed since 2026-05-24.
  Check: run 'firebase deploy --only functions' and retry. If it works with no source change, this was the cause.

Fallback hypothesis (only if a redeploy does NOT fix it): the signed-in account's 'role' custom claim is not actually 'super_admin'. Note this only fits if the message names a DIFFERENT caller role — a missing or unrecognised claim takes the earlier branch and yields 'Only admins and teachers can provision accounts' (index.ts:85) instead. So confirm the EXACT caller role in the error string before chasing this.

Follow-up regardless of cause: a deploy this stale went unnoticed because nothing verifies that the deployed function matches HEAD. Related: al_rasikhoon-5j2 (audit allUsers invoker after every deploy) — both point at a missing post-deploy verification step.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by evidence, not inference):

The TypeScript source was correct all along; the DEPLOYED JavaScript was two months stale.

Evidence:
1. Caller is genuinely super admin — auth uid dYP8antMcJWMYtn9dARCKY76NZg2 (admin@alrasikhoon.local) has custom claim {"role":"super_admin"}, and users/{uid}.role == 'super_admin'. So the 'wrong claim' hypothesis is FALSIFIED.
2. Client is correct — add_supervisor_screen.dart:101 sends role:'supervisor' + instituteId via FirebaseService.provisionUserAccount.
3. functions/src/index.ts:33 ROLES_BY_CALLER gives super_admin {supervisor, teacher, student, guardian}.
4. BUT functions/lib/index.js (the COMPILED artifact, dated May 10 — before commit 6054be7 of May 24 which added supervisor provisioning) contained:
     super_admin: new Set(["teacher", "student", "guardian"])   // no "supervisor"

Why redeploying did not help: Firebase deploys functions/lib (package.json main = lib/index.js), NOT src. firebase.json had NO 'predeploy' hook, so 'firebase deploy --only functions' never ran tsc. functions/.gitignore ignores lib/, so it is a purely local build artifact that had not been rebuilt since May 10. Every redeploy faithfully re-shipped the stale build. This is also why the error names 'super_admin' as the caller — the deployed matrix genuinely lacked that entry.

FIX:
- Rebuilt: cd functions && npm run build → compiled matrix now contains 'supervisor'. VERIFIED in lib/index.js.
- Durable guard: added the standard Firebase TS predeploy hook to firebase.json so tsc always runs before a deploy:
    "predeploy": ["npm --prefix \"$RESOURCE_DIR\" run build"]
- Still needs: firebase deploy --only functions, then re-verify the add-supervisor flow.

BLAST RADIUS — any functions/src change made between 2026-05-10 and today has NEVER been in production. Audit commits touching functions/src since May 10 (6054be7 and later) before assuming anything else works.
close_reason: Fixed and verified in production. Root cause: firebase.json had no predeploy hook, so deploys shipped a stale functions/lib/index.js (built 2026-05-10, before 6054be7 added supervisor provisioning) — the source was correct, the compiled artifact was not. title: Make createUserAccount cloud function write users/{uid} doc atomically (close orphan + TOCTOU windows)
description:
Today the createUserAccount Cloud Function only creates the Firebase Auth user; the admin/teacher client then writes users/{uid} with role/name/username/phone. This leaves three failure modes:

1. Orphan auth user. If the client crashes or loses connectivity between the function returning and the Firestore write, an auth user exists with no profile. App login returns account_not_found and the next attempt with the same username collides on email-already-in-use, stranding the original.

2. Client decides identity. The contract today is 'server creates auth, client decides role/name'. Rules permit it, but the function should be authoritative — the role passed in must match what the caller is permitted to provision (super_admin → teacher/student/guardian; teacher → student/guardian).

3. Username TOCTOU. Client pre-checks username uniqueness via UserRepository.getUserByUsername before calling the function. Two admins racing both see 'free' and both create.

Fix:
- Extend createUserAccount payload to {email, password, role, name, username, phone?}.
- Inside the function, in a transaction: query users where username == X; if exists, throw HttpsError(already-exists, 'username-taken'). Create auth user via admin.auth().createUser. Write users/{uid} with caller-permitted role + provided name/username/phone + auth_provider='email_password' + is_active=true + created_at=server timestamp. On any failure after auth.createUser, call admin.auth().deleteUser(uid) to roll back.
- Authorization matrix: super_admin can provision teacher | student | guardian. teacher can provision student | guardian (only).
- Client call sites (lib/data/services/firebase_service.dart provisionUserAccount, lib/features/admin/screens/add_teacher_screen.dart, lib/data/repositories/student_repository.dart createStudent + guardian branch) stop writing users/{uid}; they only set student/{studentId} where applicable.
- Test refactor: student_repository_test.dart no longer sees the user doc appear in FakeFirebaseFirestore (it's behind the mocked gateway). Either expand the gateway mock to also write to FakeFirebaseFirestore, or assert via verify(provisionUserAccount).called with the right payload and drop the FakeFirebaseFirestore-based user-doc asserts.
- Decision needed: keep students/{studentId} client-written (it's a separate aggregate; teacher writes are already permitted by rules) OR move that into the function too. Recommendation: keep client-written for now to avoid scope creep.

Acceptance:
- Killing the network between function return and would-have-been Firestore write leaves no orphan auth user (function succeeded → both exist; function failed → neither exists).
- Concurrent creates with same username: at most one succeeds; the other gets a clear 'username-taken' error.
- Teacher cannot provision a teacher account via the function (permission-denied).

title: Admin gets signed out and switched to new teacher/student account on creation
description:
When a super_admin creates a new Teacher (or Student), the app silently signs the admin out and signs in as the newly-created user. Repro: admin -> 'إضافة معلم' -> fill form -> submit -> admin lands inside the new teacher account.

Root cause: lib/features/admin/screens/add_teacher_screen.dart:78 calls firebaseService.createUserWithEmailPassword, which wraps FirebaseAuth.createUserWithEmailAndPassword. The Firebase Auth CLIENT SDK auto-signs-in the newly-created user, swapping the admin's active session. This cannot be opted out of on the client.

Same bug in student creation: lib/data/repositories/student_repository.dart:53 and :88.

Fix: move teacher/student creation to a Cloud Function using firebase-admin (admin.auth().createUser + Firestore users doc write), mirroring the existing setUserPassword pattern in functions/src/index.ts. Authorize via request.auth.token.role ('super_admin' creates teachers; 'super_admin' or owning teacher creates students). Client never calls createUserWithEmailAndPassword, so the caller's session stays intact.

Acceptance:
- Super admin creates a teacher and remains signed in as super admin afterwards
- Super admin / teacher creates a student and the caller remains signed in
- New users still get role custom claim via syncRoleClaim
- Username uniqueness still enforced (move check into the function)

title: auth/username-pw-10: Seed super-admin script + dev data wipe
description:
Phase 10 of the username+password migration. Depends on phase 9. Run on dev FIRST; production wipe is a separate user-approved step.

Scope:
- scripts/seed_super_admin.dart (or a Cloud Function 'seedSuperAdmin' for production): reads SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME and SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD from --dart-define / function config, creates the Firebase Auth account ('<username>@alrasikhoon.local' + password), then writes /users/<uid> with role=super_admin, username=<username>, auth_provider=email_password, name (also passed in), is_active=true, created_at=now.
- scripts/wipe_users.dart (or Cloud Function): clears the /users collection AND deletes every Firebase Auth user. Requires explicit --confirm flag plus a project-id check that refuses to run against the prod project unless --i-know-what-im-doing is also passed.
- README.md: document the bootstrap step ('After deploying for the first time, run the seed script with --dart-define=SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME=... --dart-define=SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD=...').

Production wipe (NOT part of this issue's merge):
- Filed as a follow-up, gated on user approval. The merge of this issue should leave prod untouched.

Acceptance:
- Run wipe on dev: /users is empty, Firebase Auth user pool is empty.
- Run seed on dev: a single super-admin user exists in both Firestore and Firebase Auth, can log in via the new login screen, and is redirected to /admin.
notes: Phase 10 complete. Created scripts/seed_super_admin.mjs (Firebase Admin SDK script, env-driven, creates Firebase Auth account + Firestore /users doc + role=super_admin custom claim) and scripts/wipe_users.mjs (refuses prod project unless --i-know-what-im-doing, requires --confirm, deletes Firestore /users in 400-doc batches and Auth user pool in 1000-user pages). Added scripts/package.json and scripts/README.md with documented usage. Scripts are NOT run automatically — production wipe is a deliberate user-approved manual step.

title: Deploy email-link App Link verification (fill placeholders + deploy + verify)
description:
Code/config for App Links + Universal Links is in place but not yet live. Before deploy:

1. Fill in placeholders:
   - web/.well-known/apple-app-site-association
     Replace TEAMID0000 with the real Apple Developer Team ID (10-char prefix on the bundle ID, found in Apple Developer Portal).
   - web/.well-known/assetlinks.json
     Replace RELEASE_KEYSTORE_SHA256_FINGERPRINT_PLACEHOLDER with the SHA-256 of the release signing key. Get it via:
       keytool -list -v -keystore <release.keystore> -alias <key-alias>
     The debug SHA-256 is already filled in.

2. Add the release SHA-256 fingerprint to the Firebase project console (Project Settings → Your apps → Android app → SHA certificate fingerprints), so signInWithEmailLink and Google Sign-In keep working.

3. Add 'alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/auth/email-link' to Firebase Auth's 'Authorized domains' list (Console → Authentication → Settings → Authorized domains) if not already present.

4. Build + deploy:
     flutter build web --release
     firebase deploy --only hosting

5. Verify hosting serves the files with Content-Type: application/json:
     curl -I https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/.well-known/apple-app-site-association
     curl -I https://alrasikhoon-57151.web.app/.well-known/assetlinks.json

6. Verify Apple's CDN can fetch the AASA via Apple's validator (search-developer-app-site-association API).

7. Build and install fresh app builds (iOS + Android) — Universal Links activate on first install/update; Android App Link verification happens at install time.

8. End-to-end test: request login on phone → tap link in email → app opens (not browser) → user logged in.

Closes al_rasikhoon-bye once verified end-to-end.
notes: Superseded by the username+password migration. Email-link App Link / Universal Link verification is no longer needed; the .well-known files and intent-filter will be removed in phase 8 of the migration.

title: Magic link login broken: opens web instead of app, web also fails
description:
When attempting to log in, the user receives an email containing a magic link. Two problems:

1. **Wrong target**: Clicking the link opens the web version of the app instead of the mobile app (deep linking / universal links not configured or broken).

2. **Web login also fails**: Even when redirected to the web version, the magic link does not authenticate the user — login fails on web too.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open the mobile app and request login
2. Receive the login email
3. Click the link in the email

**Expected:**
- On mobile: link should open the mobile app and log the user in
- On web: link should authenticate the user

**Actual:**
- Link opens web version (not mobile app)
- Web version fails to log the user in

**Areas to investigate:**
- Universal Links / App Links configuration (iOS associated domains, Android intent filters)
- Magic link token handler on the web side
- Token expiry / single-use enforcement
- Email template link target
notes:
Code + config work complete. Three commits on main: callback route (7d8a199), mobile deep-link domain (a133fc9), AASA/assetlinks/firebase.json (943d4e1). Bug is NOT resolved in production until al_rasikhoon-1xa is done — placeholders need real values, Firebase Hosting needs to be deployed, and the apps need to be rebuilt/reinstalled so Universal Links / App Links take effect.
Superseded by the username+password migration (plan: i-need-to-change-elegant-stardust). Magic-link flow is being removed entirely, so the deep-link / web-fallback bug no longer needs to be fixed.

title: Fix: GradeInfo.color bakes raw light-mode colors, bypasses AppTokens dark mode
description: lib/core/utils/grade_calculator.dart's GradeInfo class bakes `color: AppColors.gradeRasikh` etc. as compile-time `const` values — raw, brightness-unaware light-mode colors. AppTokens (lib/core/theme/app_tokens.dart) already has brightness-aware equivalents (tokens.gradeRasikh..gradeMuhib, added in the manuscript-UI plan al_rasikhoon-5ss Task 10) but GradeCalculator was never in that task's file list, so this was never wired up. Every screen/widget reading gradeInfo.color directly (confirmed: lib/features/student/screens/session_detail_screen.dart, lib/shared/widgets/grade_display.dart's GradeBadge) renders grade-tier colors in the wrong (light-mode) hue when the app is in dark mode — poor contrast against the dark background. Also note: AppColors.gradeMujtahid/gradeMuhib don't even match AppTokens.light's corresponding values, a separate unflagged drift. Proper fix likely means dropping color from GradeInfo entirely and having each call site map Grade -> tokens.gradeX itself (an architectural change spanning multiple files, not a one-line fix).
notes: Task 17 verification grep sweep (grep -rl app_colors lib/features/student/screens lib/shared/widgets) found this same class of bug extends beyond gradeInfo.color call sites: raw AppColors.success/AppColors.warning literals are used directly (not via gradeInfo.color) alongside adaptive tokens.maroon/tokens.green in the same pass/fail branches, in: lib/shared/widgets/error_counter.dart (lines ~130,139 pass/fail icon+text color), lib/shared/widgets/session_record_row.dart (line ~55 pass color, and _colorForStatus lines ~176-180 duration pace-coding), and lib/shared/widgets/grade_display.dart's GradeDisplay class pass/fail indicator (lines ~78,87,97 — separate from its already-known gradeInfo.color usage). tokens.green already exists and is used correctly as the adaptive 'pass'/on-target color elsewhere in session_record_row.dart (isTalqeen branch, line 54) — the natural fix for these sites is swapping AppColors.success -> tokens.green and AppColors.warning -> tokens.gold, alongside whatever fix lands for GradeInfo.color itself.

title: UI/UX overhaul: distinctive, token-driven visual identity for Al Rasikhoon (light + real dark, RTL)
description:
# UI/UX Overhaul: give Al Rasikhoon a distinctive visual identity

## Why
The app currently reads as templated Material 3: elevation-2 cards, uniform 8px radii,
raw-color palette with no token layer, and a dark theme that is a stub (`AppTheme.darkTheme`
returns `lightTheme`). For a Qur'an-memorization product used daily by students, teachers,
and supervisors, the UI should feel like it belongs to *this* subject — the discipline of
hifz, the manuscript tradition, the sense of steady progress toward mastery — not to any
generic dashboard.

## Design direction (frontend-design skill: color / type / layout / signature)
Ground every choice in the subject (Qur'an memorization, Arabic/RTL, the Rasikh→Muhib
mastery ladder). This is the brief, not final code — refine the plan before building.

- **Color**: keep the mosque-green + gold heritage but promote it into a proper token
  system (surface tints, elevation-by-color not by shadow, semantic grade colors). Derive a
  REAL dark theme (deep ink/night-prayer palette), not a copy of light.
- **Type**: `Cairo` is fine for UI/body; consider a characterful Arabic display face used
  with restraint for headings/ayah references, and a tabular/utility treatment for numbers
  (durations mm:ss, error counts, grades). Set an intentional type scale.
- **Layout**: consistent screen scaffold (RTL-correct), calmer cards, clear information
  hierarchy on the dashboards and session/recitation screens. Structure should encode the
  memorization workflow, not decorate it.
- **Signature**: one memorable element that embodies hifz progress (e.g. the mastery-ladder
  / juz progress treatment) — spend boldness here, keep everything else quiet.
- **Motion**: a small, deliberate motion system (page transitions, progress reveals,
  grade-award micro-interaction). Respect reduced-motion.

## Scope / suggested breakdown (file child issues per slice)
1. Design tokens + spacing/radius/elevation scale in `lib/core/theme` (foundation).
2. Real dark theme + system/light/dark switching (currently stubbed).
3. Shared component pass: `app_card`, `app_button`, `stat_card`, `grade_display`,
   `progress_bar`, `student_level_progress`, `bottom_nav_bar`, `role_shell`.
4. Student flow screens (dashboard, session detail/history, home practice).
5. Teacher / supervisor / admin dashboards + list screens.
6. Auth screens.
7. Motion + empty/error/loading states with in-voice copy.

## Constraints
- Arabic-first, RTL must stay correct throughout.
- `GoogleFonts.config.allowRuntimeFetching = false` — any new font must be bundled under
  `google_fonts/` (see pubspec note), not fetched at runtime.
- Follow project DDD/Clean-Architecture layering: theme/widgets are presentation-layer only.
- No behavior/logic changes — visual/UX layer only.

## Definition of done
- Coherent token-driven theme; light + working dark.
- Shared components restyled; screens adopt them.
- Motion + empty/error states in place; reduced-motion respected.
- Builds clean, screens verified on a phone viewport in both themes and in Arabic RTL.
notes: Pass 1 (foundation + student slice) COMPLETE as of Task 17 verification (2026-07-15): design tokens (AppTokens, lib/core/theme), a real brightness-aware dark theme (previously a stub returning lightTheme), a persisted theme-mode toggle (Settings screen, SharedPreferences-backed), every shared widget in lib/shared/widgets reskinned to the token system, the two signature pieces (Illuminated Juz Ring, mastery ladder), and all 4 student screens (dashboard, session detail, session history, home practice) adopting the system end-to-end. Verification: flutter analyze clean (only 3 pre-existing RawKeyEvent deprecations in app_text_field.dart, predating this plan); flutter test full suite 970 passed / 3 failed (the 3 failures are in test/e2e/teacher_flow_test.dart, confirmed pre-existing at the branch's merge-base with main, unrelated to this plan's commits — reproduced identically on a clean worktree at commit 645a5a8 before any of the 33 pass-1 commits); grep sweep for adaptive-color leaks in student screens/shared widgets found only pre-cleared exceptions (AppColors.textOnPrimary/textOnSecondary in app_button.dart and session_timer.dart, AppColors.warning/info in student_card.dart) plus confirmed additional scope for the already-filed grade-color bug (al_rasikhoon-3k3). NOT closing this epic — pass 1 is done but 5 follow-up children are now filed and linked (teacher/admin/supervisor/auth screen reskins + the grade-color dark-mode bug) for out-of-scope work this pass intentionally deferred.

title: Show recitation duration/timer on the Talqeen screen
description: The Talqeen screen currently does not display the duration/timer. Add the duration display to the Talqeen screen so the teacher can see elapsed time there, consistent with how it appears on the other recitation screens.

title: Rename session summary title to 'summary of recitation' and reposition the timer out of the nav bar
description:
Two UI changes:

1. Title: Change the summary screen title from 'summary of the session' to 'summary of recitation'.

2. Timer placement: Move the recitation timer out of the navigation bar to a better location. Exact placement/design is open (TBD) — aim for how a modern app would present an in-progress timer (e.g. a floating pill/badge, a prominent header element, or a dedicated status area). Explore options and pick a clean, modern treatment.

title: Improve recitation duration display: color-code pace, change/remove 'أقصر من المستهدف' text, show seconds
description:
Rework how session/part duration is displayed for the teacher.

Changes:
1. Text: Change the 'أقصر من المستهدف' (shorter than target) label to something better, or remove it entirely — the color coding should convey the meaning instead.
2. Color-code the duration by pace vs. target:
   - On time = green
   - Faster than target = yellow
   - Beyond target (slower/over) = red
3. Show seconds, not only minutes (e.g. mm:ss instead of just minutes).

title: Consolidate student info into student profile screen (embed session history)
description:
When a teacher clicks on a student, open the student's profile screen showing everything about that student in one place instead of scattered across tabs.

The student profile screen should display:
- Student name
- Username
- Level details
- Progress
- Pace
- Session history (currently in a separate dedicated tab)
- Current session
- Ability to start the session from this screen

Goal: Move session history out of its separate dedicated teacher tab and embed it inside the student profile screen, so the teacher can see the full picture of a student — identity, level, progress, pace, history, and current session — on a single screen and start the session from there.

title: Recitation summary: show pass/fail only, detailed per-part results with recited content
description:
The recitation summary screen currently shows the evaluation category (hafez, motqen, etc). This should be removed. The summary should only show the overall result: Naje7 (pass) or not.

The detailed result should be broken down per part (new, recent, distance). Each part should show:
- its own detailed evaluation result
- the content the student recited for that part

title: Remove intermediate per-part result screens from recitation flow
description: Teachers evaluating a recitation currently pass through a per-part result screen (grade for الحفظ الجديد / المراجعة القريبة / المراجعة البعيدة) between each evaluation screen. Remove these intermediate result screens so 'التالي' on one evaluation screen goes straight to the next evaluation screen, and the last part goes directly to the session summary. Per-part grades and pass/fail remain visible on the final session summary. Deletes recitation_result_screen.dart and the recitationResult route.

title: main is red: session_detail_shell_locality_test doesn't mock the Hive session box
description: On origin/main (since the al_rasikhoon-3hn merge, commit 02b065f/2927c01), test/unit/routing/session_detail_shell_locality_test.dart fails all 3 tests with 'UnimplementedError: session box not opened'. Its setUp builds container.read(routerProvider), which now transitively reads sessionCacheProvider -> sessionBoxProvider (lib/data/services/session_cache.dart:41) from the fast-startup work, but the test never opens or overrides the Hive box. Fix: in the test's ProviderContainer overrides, override sessionBoxProvider (or sessionCacheProvider) with a fake/in-memory box, mirroring test/unit/services/session_cache_test.dart. Confirmed failing on plain main (+855 -3) independent of the session-duration-timer feature (which merged clean, adding 34 passing tests, 0 new failures). Discovered while merging al_rasikhoon-tr6.

title: Admin: tapping a student's session-history record jumps into the student shell
description:
Repro: as super_admin, open a student (/admin/students/:id -> StudentProgressScreen) -> Session History section -> tap a record. The app switches to the whole student screen instead of showing the record in the admin context.

Root cause: lib/shared/screens/student_progress_screen.dart:466-468 (_SessionHistoryList onTap) pushes AppRoutes.sessionDetail = '/student/history/:recordId', which lives in the STUDENT StatefulShellRoute. Pushing it from the admin shell crosses shells, so go_router lands the user in the student shell.

Precedent/fix direction: the teacher flow avoids this with a shell-local route (AppRoutes.teacherSessionDetail = '/teacher/history/:recordId', registered in the teacher shell -> SessionDetailScreen). Admin has no equivalent. Add an admin-shell session-detail route (e.g. AppRoutes.adminStudentSessionDetail under the admin Home/students branch in lib/routing/app_router.dart) and have StudentProgressScreen push the route matching the ACTIVE shell instead of the hardcoded student route. Note the same screen is reused for supervisor (supervisorStudentProgress) — verify the supervisor path doesn't hit the same cross-shell jump; if it does, cover it too.

Related: cross-shell duplicate-page-key crash history (#45) and the al_rasikhoon-801 role-shell work.
close_reason: Injected shell-local session-detail route into StudentProgressScreen for admin and supervisor shells; tapping a history record now stays in the active shell instead of jumping to the student shell. Added regression tests.

title: Talqeen counter tap flashes a spinner over the next-passage card
description: On NextContentTalqeenScreen, changing either recitation count (repetitionsWithTeacher / homeRepetitionsRequired) mutates activeSessionProvider. activeSessionNextMeetingProvider watches the WHOLE activeSessionProvider state, so the unrelated counter change invalidates it and its .when() in the screen flashes a CircularProgressIndicator over the passage card on every tap. Fix: subscribe only to meeting + studentId via .select.

title: Session duration timer
description: Time every session start->save, record duration on the record, show it + over/under-target flag on the log card, and a live in-session timer. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-15-session-duration-timer.md

title: Session detail screen shows the raw curriculum id as its title
description: The student's session detail screen printed record.curriculumSessionId (e.g. L1_J30_S1) as the card heading. That id is a key, not a name — a student should read the session's Arabic title (تلقين - الجزء 30 / الحلقة 5 - الجزء 30 / the assessment's verbatim label). Resolve the session via the curriculum repo and render SessionModel.titleAr, falling back to the id only if the session cannot be resolved.

title: [bug] Emulator harness: _withSeedRole can't restore super_admin, so addStudentRecord always hits permission-denied
description:
Found while driving the paced-curriculum feature against the Firebase emulator (al_rasikhoon-g63).

integration_test/helpers/firebase_emulator_app.dart::_withSeedRole is broken against the CURRENT firestore.rules. It is a one-way door:

    await ref.update({'role': role});     // super_admin -> teacher : ALLOWED (isSuperAdmin passes)
    try { await body(); }
    finally { await ref.update({'role': 'super_admin'}); }   // teacher -> super_admin : DENIED

The users update rule is:
    allow update: if isSuperAdmin() ||
      (request.auth.uid == userId &&
       request.resource.data.role == resource.data.role &&
       request.resource.data.institute_id == resource.data.institute_id);

Once the seed account has demoted itself to 'teacher', isSuperAdmin() is false, and the self-update branch FREEZES the role field. So the restore in the finally block throws permission-denied.

IMPACT: any emulator-backed integration test that calls env.addStudentRecord() (the only caller of _withSeedRole) fails with [cloud_firestore/permission-denied]. That includes integration_test/firebase_emulator_flow_test.dart.

Reproduce:
  firebase emulators:start --only auth,firestore
  flutter test integration_test/firebase_emulator_flow_test.dart -d <IOS_SIM_ID>

The role-freeze rule is deliberate (it stops a supervisor self-promoting to super_admin — see the rule's own comment), so the RULE is right and the HARNESS is wrong.

Possible fixes:
  a) Don't round-trip the role at all: seed students through the emulator's admin REST endpoint (which bypasses rules), the way clearEmulatorFirestore already does.
  b) Keep two seed identities — one super_admin, one teacher — and pick the right one per collection, instead of mutating one account's role.
  c) Order the writes so every super_admin write happens BEFORE a one-way demotion to teacher, and never restore. (This is the workaround I used to get the verification run through.)

(a) is the cleanest: seeding should not be subject to the rules it is seeding around.

title: [bug] Teachers can write session and sard records for students who aren't theirs
description:
firestore.rules gates session_records create/update on a bare isTeacher(), with no check that the record's student belongs to that teacher. Supervisors, by contrast, are institute-scoped via isSupervisorOfRecordStudent(). After al_rasikhoon-801, sard_records inherits the same bare isTeacher() rule.

So any authenticated teacher can create or edit a session/sard record for ANY student in the system, including students of another teacher in another institute — fabricating or altering someone else's progress and advancement.

Not introduced by al_rasikhoon-801: the hole already exists on session_records, and the swap simply gives sard_records the same (existing) teacher rule rather than inventing a looser one.

Fix: scope teacher record writes to the teacher's own students, e.g. an isTeacherOfRecordStudent(recordData) helper mirroring isSupervisorOfRecordStudent — with the same dual-check on update (old AND new student) so a record's student_id cannot be repointed to another teacher's student.

Related to the P0 rules work in al_rasikhoon-bpk (records are also world-readable). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-14-sard-teacher-exam-supervisor-design.md.

title: 3 pre-existing failures in supervisor_flow_test (exam screen viewport/tap)
description: integration_test/supervisor_flow_test.dart: 3 exam-result failures — a call to tap() derives an off-screen Offset in exam_session_screen.dart. Independently reproduced on pre-branch code (worktree at d5de79c), so NOT caused by the talqeen branch. Needs a scroll/viewport fix in the exam session screen.

title: The recitation session screen overflows on a phone (same defect as the اختبار screen)
description:
lib/features/teacher/screens/recitation_screen.dart lays its body out as a plain, non-scrollable Column + Spacer (line ~86), exactly like ExamSessionScreen did before #59. Inside the teacher StatefulShellRoute the viewport is the phone minus the status bar, the app bar, the bottom nav and the home indicator; once the header card, the error counter and the action row exceed that height, the Spacer collapses to zero and the bottom overflows, putting the action buttons out of reach.

Not yet observed overflowing only because the recitation header is shorter than the اختبار one — it will on a smaller phone or with larger accessibility text.

Fix as in #59 (commit 44a28e4): wrap the body in SafeArea -> CustomScrollView -> SliverFillRemaining(hasScrollBody: false) around the same Column. Cover it with a widget test modelled on test/widget/exam_session_overflow_test.dart, which pumps the screen inside a shell Scaffold with the real AppBottomNavBar and device insets.
close_reason: Fixed. lib/features/teacher/screens/recitation_screen.dart:103-107 applies the same SafeArea->CustomScrollView->SliverFillRemaining scroll fix.

title: The سرد session screen overflows on a phone (same defect as the اختبار screen)
description:
lib/features/supervisor/screens/sard_session_screen.dart lays its body out as a plain, non-scrollable Column + Spacer (line ~57), exactly like ExamSessionScreen did before #59. The screen renders inside the supervisor StatefulShellRoute, so its viewport is the phone minus the status bar, the app bar, the bottom nav and the home indicator. Once the header card, the error counter and the end-session button exceed that height, the Spacer collapses to zero and the bottom overflows — the action button becomes unreachable.

It has not been seen overflowing yet only because the سرد header is shorter than the اختبار one, but it will on a smaller phone or with larger accessibility text.

Fix as in #59 (commit 44a28e4): wrap the body in SafeArea -> CustomScrollView -> SliverFillRemaining(hasScrollBody: false) around the same Column, which scrolls when the content does not fit yet still lets the Spacer pin the button to the bottom when it does. Cover it with a widget test modelled on test/widget/exam_session_overflow_test.dart, which pumps the screen inside a shell Scaffold with the real AppBottomNavBar and device insets.
close_reason: Fixed. lib/features/teacher/screens/sard_session_screen.dart:60-63 now wraps the Column in SafeArea->CustomScrollView->SliverFillRemaining(hasScrollBody:false) (mirrors #59/44a28e4).

title: Paced curriculum (NX): let a fast memorizer run at 2x, 3x or N×
description:
A gifted student who memorizes quickly is held to the average student's pace of one session per meeting. Let a teacher or supervisor set a per-student pace N so a meeting covers N lessons.

The hard part was the two review streams, and the curriculum data answers it. Recent review is a sliding window — recent(K) = new(K-2) ∪ new(K-1), verified 659/672 across all ten levels. Distant review is a cursor that sweeps everything already memorized, one non-overlapping chunk per session.

So distant concatenates cleanly under NX, but recent does NOT: unioning two rows' recent blocks pulls in new content taught in the same meeting. Recent must instead be the new content of the previous 2N sessions.

Nothing is invented — every range in a composed meeting is a range the source curriculum already states. At N=1 the rule reproduces the authored curriculum byte-for-byte, so 1x students are provably unaffected.

Only lessons batch: تلقين, سرد and اختبار each always stand alone.

Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-14-paced-curriculum-nx-design.md

title: Admin: supervisors screen (mirroring teachers) + show teachers AND supervisors on the institute detail screen
description:
Two related gaps in the admin area.

PART 1 — Admin supervisors screen.
There is no supervisors list. lib/features/admin/screens/ has teachers_screen.dart and teacher_detail_screen.dart, but no supervisors_screen.dart / supervisor_detail_screen.dart. Supervisors are currently created into a void: allSupervisorsProvider (admin_provider.dart:63, backed by userRepository.getSupervisors()) ALREADY EXISTS but is READ BY NOTHING — its only reference is add_supervisor_screen.dart:108 invalidating it after a create. So an admin can add a supervisor and then never see them again.
Build a supervisors screen mirroring teachers_screen.dart, wired to the existing allSupervisorsProvider, reachable from the admin dashboard, with a detail view mirroring teacher_detail_screen.dart.

PART 2 — Institute detail should list teachers AND supervisors.
institute_detail_screen.dart currently watches teachersForInstituteProvider only (admin_provider.dart:87) and offers add/remove teacher. It shows no supervisors.
The repository layer is already there — InstituteRepository has assignSupervisorToInstitute, removeSupervisorFromInstitute, getSupervisorIdsForInstitute, and getInstitutesForSupervisor. What is missing is a supervisorsForInstituteProvider (the teachers one exists) and the UI section.

DESIGN CONFLICT TO SETTLE FIRST — do not just copy the teacher pattern.
Teachers and supervisors have DIFFERENT relationships to an institute:
- A teacher is assigned/unassigned to institutes freely (assignTeacherToInstitute / removeTeacherFromInstitute), and getInstitutesForTeacher implies a teacher may span several.
- A supervisor is bound to EXACTLY ONE institute AT CREATION. createUserAccount (functions/src/index.ts) requires instituteId for role 'supervisor', writes it to users/{uid}.institute_id, AND creates supervisor_institutes/{uid}_{instituteId}. Per AgDR-0003, users/{uid}.institute_id is the single source of truth for supervisor scoping — it is what supervisorInstituteIdProvider and the Firestore rules use.

So if the institute detail screen offers add/remove supervisor using assignSupervisorToInstitute, it writes supervisor_institutes WITHOUT updating users/{uid}.institute_id — desyncing the assignment from the source of truth. The supervisor would appear on the institute screen while their actual scoping (students they can see, what they can provision) still points at the old institute.

Decide before building:
(a) Is institute detail READ-ONLY for supervisors (just list them), with re-binding done elsewhere? — safest, and consistent with 'bound at creation'; or
(b) Can an admin genuinely MOVE a supervisor between institutes? If so, that move must update users/{uid}.institute_id and supervisor_institutes together (atomically), and assignSupervisorToInstitute/removeSupervisorFromInstitute as they stand are NOT sufficient — they are a latent desync bug.

Related: al_rasikhoon-ecv (supervisor's own institute-scoped navigation).
notes:
DESIGN CONFLICT RESOLVED: supervisors get full teacher parity — freely assigned/unassigned, and may belong to SEVERAL institutes at once. So institute detail DOES get add/remove supervisor (option b, extended to many-to-many).

But that is blocked on al_rasikhoon-3n6, which retires users/{uid}.institute_id as the scoping source of truth and moves it to supervisor_institutes membership. Until 3n6 lands, wiring add/remove supervisor to assignSupervisorToInstitute would desync the assignment from users/{uid}.institute_id, which the Firestore rules still enforce against — the supervisor would appear on the new institute while still being scoped to the old one.

Safe to build BEFORE 3n6: Part 1 (the supervisors LIST screen + detail, wired to the already-existing-but-unread allSupervisorsProvider) and the READ-ONLY supervisors section on institute detail. Only the add/remove controls need to wait.

title: Supervisor: direct navigation to students, teachers, and assessments
description:
Requirement (translated from Arabic): the supervisor's interface must be easy and fast — the supervisor can reach students, teachers, and assessments directly, without going through complicated steps.

SCOPING RULE (settled): a supervisor sees only the students of their OWN institute. This is already the implemented behaviour on the students side — supervisorStudentsProvider scopes to users/{uid}.institute_id and calls StudentRepository.getStudentsForInstitute(), which filters where('institute_id', isEqualTo: ...). See AgDR-0003, which treats users/{uid}.institute_id as the single source of truth for scoping. The SAME rule must apply to the new teachers view.

Current state (why this is not just a polish task):
- The supervisor dashboard (lib/features/supervisor/screens/supervisor_dashboard_screen.dart) surfaces exactly ONE destination: a 'قائمة الاختبارات' (exam queue) card. Everything else is stats tiles.
- SupervisorStudentsScreen exists, is routed (lib/routing/app_router.dart:295), and is correctly institute-scoped — but NOTHING on the dashboard links to it, so it is unreachable in the running app.
- There is NO supervisor-facing teachers screen at all. The only teachers list is admin-only (lib/features/admin/screens/teachers_screen.dart) and is NOT institute-scoped — reusing it as-is would leak teachers from other institutes to a supervisor.
- There is no BottomNavigationBar / NavigationBar anywhere under lib/features/supervisor, so there is no persistent way to switch sections.

Of the three destinations the requirement asks for: assessments is reachable, students is orphaned, teachers does not exist.

Scope:
- Give the supervisor a persistent top-level navigation with three sections: Students / Teachers / Assessments.
- Wire the existing SupervisorStudentsScreen into that navigation so it becomes reachable. No change to its scoping — it is already correct.
- Add a supervisor teachers view scoped to users/{uid}.institute_id, mirroring the students path (a getTeachersForInstitute-style query). Do NOT reuse the admin teachers screen unscoped.
- Each section reachable in one tap from anywhere in the supervisor area.

Acceptance criteria ('easy and fast', restated as something testable):
- From any supervisor screen, each of Students / Teachers / Assessments is reachable in a single tap.
- No supervisor screen is orphaned (routed but unreachable from the UI).
- The teachers view returns only teachers whose institute_id matches the supervisor's; add a test with two institutes proving no cross-institute leakage.
- Every nav destination works, not just the first — see related bug al_rasikhoon-256 (teacher nav bar: only the first button responds). Add a test that exercises EVERY destination.

Remaining open question: should Firestore security rules enforce the teacher-list institute scoping server-side, or is the provider-level query filter considered sufficient? (Query-level filtering alone is not an access control boundary.)

title: Supervisor can edit a student's starting point, but only before the student has started
description:
Today the starting point is set once, at student creation (StartingPointPicker in lib/features/teacher/widgets/starting_point_picker.dart, used by add_student_screen.dart), and is stored as StudentModel.enrollmentPosition. After that it is immutable. A supervisor who mis-places a student has no way to correct it.

Allow a supervisor — and only a supervisor — to edit the starting point of a student who has NOT started yet.

Definition of 'has not started': the student has zero progress records of any kind. That means no session records, no sard records, and no exam records (see SessionRepository: getSessionRecordsForStudent / getSardRecordsForStudent / getExamRecordsForStudent). This is the invariant that makes the edit safe: with no records, moving the anchor cannot orphan or invalidate history, so it is a pure re-derivation.

Behaviour:
- Supervisor opens an enrolled student and, if the student has not started, can re-pick the starting point using the existing StartingPointPicker.
- Saving moves enrollmentPosition and re-derives the position fields from the new anchor exactly as enrollment does today: current_level, current_juz, current_session, current_session_id, current_session_kind, current_session_tier, current_order_in_level, completed_levels, unlocked_levels.
- If the student HAS started, the edit is not offered, and the write is rejected server-side too (do not rely on the UI alone to enforce the invariant — a stale screen must not be able to move a student who started in the meantime).
- Anyone other than a supervisor is rejected.

Scope note: the case where a student HAS already started is deliberately NOT covered here — that is the harder problem (recompute progress, decide the fate of existing session records) and is tracked separately in al_rasikhoon-i1d. This issue is the safe subset of that.

Open questions to settle during design:
- Where does this live in the supervisor UI (student detail screen? a dedicated edit screen?).
- Is the enforcement point a Firestore security rule, a Cloud Function, or both?
- Should the move be audited (who moved whom, from where to where)?

Depends on the placement model in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-13-flexible-student-starting-point-design.md.

title: Teacher screen: only the first nav bar button works
description:
On the teacher screen, tapping any nav bar item other than the first one does nothing — the screen does not change. Only the first nav bar button navigates correctly.

Repro: open the teacher screen, tap each nav bar item in turn.
Expected: each nav item switches to its corresponding screen.
Actual: only the first item responds; the rest are unresponsive.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (investigated on worktree off origin/main):

The teacher bottom nav renders 4 items (bottom_nav_bar.dart:82-104 — الطلاب/الحلقة/السجل/الإعدادات) but the teacher StatefulShellRoute declares only ONE StatefulShellBranch, the Students branch (app_router.dart:341-400).

RoleShell (role_shell.dart:28) guards with 'if (index >= branchCount) return;' so taps on indexes 1-3 are silently swallowed — no navigation, no error. Hence only the first button works.

Not a regression: الحلقة / السجل / الإعدادات screens for the teacher were never implemented or routed.

SAME LATENT MISMATCH IN OTHER ROLES:
- supervisor: 4 nav items, 3 branches → الإعدادات is a dead button
- student/guardian: 4 nav items, 3 branches → الإعدادات is a dead button
- superAdmin: 4 items, 4 branches → OK

FIX OPTIONS:
(a) Implement + route the missing teacher branches (real fix, needs 3 screens).
(b) Derive nav items from navigationShell.route.branches so the bar can never show a tab that has no branch (kills the whole class of bug).
(c) Both — (b) as a guardrail, (a) as the feature work.

title: Teacher list card overflows horizontally on a long contact string
description:
RenderFlex overflow of ~8.9px on the teacher list (admin > Teachers).

Cause: lib/features/admin/screens/teachers_screen.dart:60 — the inner Row holding the contact line has an unconstrained Text at :70 (`teacher.phone ?? teacher.email`). The Row is inside an Expanded/Column that gives it only ~181.5px, and neither the Text nor the Row lets the child shrink, so a long contact string overruns instead of ellipsizing. Teachers with no phone fall back to the synthesized email (username@<synthesizedEmailDomain>), which is easily long enough to overflow. A long teacher list just makes it likelier that some teacher hits the case; the row count itself is not the trigger.

Fix: wrap the contact Text in Expanded with overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis (and maxLines: 1). Check the teacher name Text at :55 for the same issue — a long name has no ellipsis either.

Repro: open the admin Teachers screen with a teacher who has no phone number and a long username; the yellow/black overflow stripes appear on the card.

Log:
A RenderFlex overflowed by 8.9 pixels on the right.
The relevant error-causing widget was: Row Row:.../lib/features/admin/screens/teachers_screen.dart:60:29
constraints: BoxConstraints(0.0<=w<=181.5, 0.0<=h<=Infinity), size: Size(181.5, 16.0), direction: horizontal, textDirection: rtl
close_reason: Fixed. teachers_screen.dart:77-86 wraps contact Text in Expanded + maxLines:1 + ellipsis (name at :57-62 too); covered by test/widget/teachers_screen_overflow_test.dart (green).

title: Represent 'finished the curriculum' instead of parking the student in the exam queue
description: After passing the level-10 / hizb-2 final exam, advanceStudentSession credits level 10 but leaves current_session at 36 (terminal by design). getStudentsReadyForExam queries current_session == 36 and StudentModel.canTakeExam is currentSession == 36, so the student stays in the supervisor's exam queue forever and can be re-examined indefinitely. Needs a product decision on what 'finished' looks like (a completed flag, a graduation state, exclusion from the queue). Found by the final whole-branch review of feat/flexible-student-starting-point.
